content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, denominations less than rupees 101010 have stopped and now rupees 101010 is the smallest denomination.

Suppose Chef goes to buy some item with cost **not** a multiple of 101010, then, he will be charged the cost that is the **nearest multiple** of 101010.

If the cost is equally distant from two nearest multiples of 101010, then the cost is **rounded up**.

For example, 35,38,40,4435, 38, 40, 4435,38,40,44 are all rounded to 404040.

Chef purchased an item having cost XXX (X≤100)(X \leq 100)(X≤100) and gave a bill of rupees 100100100. How much amount will he get back?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single integer XXX, the cost of the item.


Output Format
For each test case, output the amount returned to Chef.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 100) – the original cost of the item.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the amount of money Chef receives back after rounding the cost to the nearest multiple of 10 (rounding up on a tie) and paying with a 100‑rupee note.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each X we round to the nearest multiple of 10 (up on tie) and subtract from 100. 35→40 (change 60), 54→50 (change 50), 80→80 (change 20), 12→10 (change 90).
5 is exactly halfway, round up to 10 → change 90. 47 rounds up to 50 → change 50. 99 rounds up to 100 → change 0.

solution.approach: For each cost X we need the nearest multiple of 10. Let r = X % 10. If r < 5 we round down to X - r. If r > 5 we round up to X + (10 - r). When r == 5 the distance to the lower and upper multiples is equal, and the statement requires rounding up, so we round to X + 5. The change returned is 100 - roundedCost. This is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
